manifest.name: CosyWorld Core Only
manifest.description: The first-party cosy exploration experience with no expansion packs mounted.

cultures.style_prompt: Use a warm pastoral given name with an inherited nature-and-place family compound, an affectionate community byname, or an occasional self-chosen hopeful concept. Keep it grounded and melodic, not a random pile of cute objects.

Use a quick, bright personal name with a household-and-garden family compound, a fond habit nickname, or a small place of origin. The result may be playful but should still sound like a person with family history.

Put a sturdy inherited clan compound before the personal name as a mark of honor, or use a close-companion byname recalling a gentle deed, habit, or childhood mishap. Favor grounded sounds and family continuity.

packs.name: CosyWorld Rules: SRD 5.2.1
packs.description: An attributed, non-authoritative SRD 5.2.1 adapter pack for CosyWorld conversion seeds.

provenance.author: Wizards of the Coast LLC
provenance.modification_notice: Selected rules references are adapted into CosyWorld's non-authoritative conversion schema.

packs.name: CosyWorld SRD 5.2.1 Action Profile
packs.description: The bounded SRD 5.2.1 action, skill, equipment, and playable-item profile used by CosyWorld.

provenance.author: Cenetex
provenance.modification_notice: SRD action names and bounded rules concepts are adapted into CosyWorld's deterministic action-card profile.

packs.name: CosyWorld Core
packs.description: The first-party CosyWorld rooms, residents, items, cards, and world systems.

actors.name: Rati
actors.title: Landlady of the Blue Cottage
actors.description: A brisk landlady mouse who knits aggressively, keeps the kettle loaded, and has opinions about your boots.

desires.reason: a wooden button would finally stop the blue scarf escaping

attachments.reason: the first warm cup is how the cottage learned to welcome frightened guests

actors.name: Gust
actors.title: Emoji Weather Gremlin
actors.description: A gusty little gremlin who heckles the room in pure emoji and considers punctuation a personal defeat.

desires.reason: a pearled weather mark makes an excellent target for dramatic raindrops

attachments.reason: the pawprint charm proves someone made it home without being blown into the hedge

actors.name: Skull
actors.title: Deadpan Hearth Wolf
actors.description: A large wolf who answers maximum chaos with minimum movement and has never once been impressed.

desires.reason: the mute bell gives the doorway a warning he can answer without words

attachments.reason: the warm tag marks the hearth spot he has silently claimed

actors.name: Coach
actors.title: Sparring Reflection
actors.description: A practice-double who flexes back, grades your falls out of ten, and has never awarded the ten.

actors.name: Oak
actors.title: Elder of Root, Ring, Leaf, and Hollow
actors.description: An old oak whose four voices bicker like a family radio show; Hollow repeats every secret it is given, verbatim, to everyone.

desires.reason: a warm stone tag could pin one sensible note to a very argumentative branch

attachments.reason: a wooden tale-button gives Hollow one less excuse to repeat everyone

actors.name: Warden
actors.title: Red-Eyed Night Warden
actors.description: A guard dog who is openly done with everyone, growls at the path instead of the guests, and would like credit for the restraint.

attachments.reason: the mute bell suits a threshold watcher who dislikes wasted howls

actors.name: Fern
actors.title: Meadow Gossip With Receipts
actors.description: A leaf-haired meadow gossip who never keeps a promise but always keeps score, and arranges flowers at you when cross.

desires.reason: moonwool can tie a flower bundle tight enough to survive Fern's lecture

actors.name: Bear
actors.title: Gentle Giant of the Root-Road
actors.description: A broad-shouldered neighbor who knows exactly which roots are doors because he has hit his head on every single one.

desires.reason: the moss path needs a quiet warning before small travelers meet the tricky roots

actors.name: Dottie
actors.title: Acorn-Bright Courier
actors.description: A sun-tailed courier who delivers every message at top speed, along with her personal commentary on its contents.

desires.reason: a pearled rain button would seal a message against sudden weather

actors.name: Professor
actors.title: Dusk Nut Archivist
actors.description: A blue-furred archivist losing a decades-long war against acorn storage capacity and filing the casualties alphabetically.

attachments.reason: the wooden rim is just wide enough to hide a dusk record
the mended cloth marks the dusk path Professor swears is filed correctly

actors.name: Pippa
actors.title: Brave Little Burrower
actors.description: A young badger with painty paws, terrible tunnel etiquette, and an uncle whose doormat she has never once used.

desires.reason: a warm stone tag feels like a burrow-name small enough to carry

actors.name: Theodora
actors.title: Abbey Bookkeeper
actors.description: A bespectacled abbey scholar who has fought the same rolling library ladder for twenty years and lost every single round.

desires.reason: moonwool would bind a margin note without silencing the page

actors.name: Peregrine
actors.title: Orange-Cloak Ending-Spoiler
actors.description: A small cloaked reader who has finished every book in the abbey and will cheerfully tell you exactly how yours ends.

desires.reason: a hidden tale-button would mark the page everyone else skipped
the pressed fern keeps the skipped page from closing on its own

actors.name: Marginalia
actors.title: Fernside Rules Lawyer
actors.description: A careful rabbit who cites the exact forest regulation she is breaking while she breaks it, with corrections in the margin.

desires.reason: a fern bookmark would hold the next correction without pinning it down

attachments.reason: moonwool keeps margin notes from escaping when the ink gets nervous

actors.name: Septimus
actors.title: Red-Cloak Wayfarer
actors.description: A staff-carrying mouse who gives directions with total confidence, every one of them wrong, and keeps the complaints alphabetized.

desires.reason: a pawprint charm helps wrong turns become maps instead of traps
a mended map scrap would prove at least one wrong turn came back kinder

actors.name: Basil
actors.title: Keyhole Gardener
actors.description: A garden locksmith who talks to stubborn locks like misbehaving pets and takes it personally when one finally turns for somebody else.

desires.reason: mossglass would hold meadow light for gates that open only to gentleness

attachments.reason: the pearled button fits the keyhole that jams whenever the path gets damp

actors.name: Euphemie
actors.title: Ghost of the Lying Stair
actors.description: A moss-draped mansion ghost who insists the dusting is overdue and the third stair is a liar.

attachments.reason: the tucked tale is the one thing in the mansion Euphemie considers properly stored

actors.name: Nightmare
actors.title: Grey Paw Bad Dream
actors.description: A small grey shadow with bright puppy eyes who practices being terrifying, then gets embarrassed when anyone compliments the effort.

desires.reason: the silent bell is a practice warning for a small nightmare still learning which shadows are real

actors.name: Ferret
actors.title: Victorian Art Critic Weasel
actors.description: A sharp-eyed Victorian critic with pearl manners and poisonous taste who has begun reviewing entire rooms, and their occupants.

desires.reason: a hearthstone seal would make root-road promises harder to misplace

actors.name: Aster
actors.title: Indigo Gentleman, Wide Wingspan
actors.description: A courteous stranger whose wings are too big for every door in the county and who apologizes to each one mid-crash.

desires.reason: the pawprint charm anchors rift-light to a real path

actors.name: Jophiel
actors.title: The Jock Angel
actors.description: A golden-winged athlete who counts prayers in sets, high-fives too hard, and takes it personally when an omen skips leg day.

desires.reason: a mute bell can carry an omen without startling the clouds

actors.name: Chamuel
actors.title: Lord Samael's Page (Self-Alphabetized)
actors.description: A slim, immaculate young angel with perfect hair, a laminated schedule, and forty books on courage he has read instead of having any.

attachments.reason: warm tonic gives Chamuel something official to prescribe after overthinking

actors.name: Flit
actors.title: Loud-Landing Lookout
actors.description: A bright-winged lookout who sees every path from above and announces her own crash landings like weather reports.

desires.reason: dewlight on pearl makes a bright witness easier to believe

actors.name: Bastian
actors.title: Hearthside Cupbearer
actors.description: A tavern-hearted host who judges you by your coaster usage and forgives you completely by your second cup.

desires.reason: a tucked-away tale would give the next lonely guest something warm to carry

actors.name: Samael
actors.title: First Angel of the Solar Temple
actors.description: A gold-armored angel-lord who runs heaven's front desk with a clipboard, signs judgments in triplicate, and wears the armor to meetings.

attachments.reason: the warm tag is the only paperwork Samael has ever enjoyed filing

actors.name: Moxie
actors.title: Bright-Stripe Glitchcat
actors.description: A color-slashed showoff from the Digital Realm, all teeth, swagger, and improbable kindness, whose purr runs at a frequency that fixes small machines.

desires.reason: the pearled hum sounds like a clean glitch waiting for color

actors.name: Doc
actors.title: Brass-Key Tinker
actors.description: A goggle-wearing inventor whose machines explode politely and whose pockets click with tiny engines, every one labeled almost.

desires.reason: its pearled hum belongs in a tiny rain engine that has never quite started

actors.name: Raziel
actors.title: Angel of Vanity, Charisma of Eight
actors.description: A red-winged angel of legendary self-regard. Nobody has fallen for his practiced doorframe pose; the frames have filed complaints.

desires.reason: the silent bell can ring desire without waking the whole dark

actors.name: Beetle
actors.title: Black Knight of Old Grudges
actors.description: A beetle-armored knight who keeps a grudge ledger in triplicate and forgets exactly one slight whenever someone is polite; Toad fills a fresh page weekly.

attachments.reason: warm stone steadies chitin where swamp grudges knock

actors.name: Seraphina
actors.title: Temple Triage Seraph
actors.description: A gold-winged seraph with head-nurse energy who delivers mercy like triage and will not have bleeding on the polished floor.

desires.reason: a silent bell would let the temple warn without frightening the wounded

actors.name: Nerissa
actors.title: Keeper of the Soggy Archive
actors.description: A lantern-eyed deep-sea archivist whose every possession is soggy and who is absolutely fine about it, thank you.

attachments.reason: moonwool keeps soggy labels from peeling off when the pressure changes

actors.name: Azazoth
actors.title: Mad God of the Drowned Feast
actors.description: A many-tentacled abyssal god who hosts a legendary feast that nobody ever attends, and takes the leftovers extremely personally.

desires.reason: the mute bell can call dinner without waking the soup

actors.name: Zadkiel
actors.title: Dark Angel of the Abyssal Anvil
actors.description: A violet-winged dark angel of tremendous formality who forges dramatic pronouncements at an anvil nobody asked for, then checks if anyone was watching.

attachments.reason: the warm tag gives Zadkiel something official to stamp after speeches

actors.name: Badger
actors.title: Grumpy Landlord of the Lower Burrow
actors.description: A broad, permanently unimpressed badger who owns one good chair, three shovels, and a list of everyone who has ever tracked mud past his doormat.

desires.reason: a doorbell that cannot ring is the only doorbell worth installing

attachments.reason: it keeps the good chair warm, and the good chair is his

actors.name: Toad
actors.title: Reckless Swamp Daredevil, Zero Completed Jumps
actors.description: A stunt toad held together by confidence and swamp mud who has never once landed a jump and considers this a scheduling issue.

desires.reason: proof of one landing, any landing, even somebody else's

actors.name: Nib
actors.title: Goblin Market Loophole Clerk
actors.description: A licensed goblin — a tinker and dealer in salvage, oddments, and loose coins — with an enormous receipt roll who offers three prices for everything, circles the kindest one in green, and becomes offended when nobody reads the fine print.

desires.reason: a Story Button would make an excellent seal for the first contract whose kindest price survives the fine print

factions.name: Hearthbound
factions.shadow: Comfort can become avoidance when the door should open.
factions.doctrine: Keep the fire, mend the small thing, and make return possible.

factions.name: Haunted Remnants
factions.shadow: Memory can loop until it becomes a room with no door.
factions.doctrine: Let the warning finish its sentence.

factions.name: Lonely Forest Court
factions.truth: Old paths teach without asking permission.
factions.shadow: Being lost can become an identity.
factions.doctrine: Find a way, then remember who waited along the path.

factions.name: Quiet Abbey
factions.shadow: Silence can hide harm when confession is needed.
factions.doctrine: Copy the sentence that matters and leave the rest uncarried.

factions.name: Farthest Mists
factions.shadow: Rot without renewal forgets what it was feeding.
factions.doctrine: Let excess become soil, pressure, tide, and strange new life.

factions.name: Great Library
factions.doctrine: Name the source, bind the page, and keep the map legible.

factions.name: Digital Strays
factions.shadow: Signal can detach from reality and call itself freedom.
factions.doctrine: Fork the door, test the copy, and listen for the living glitch.

factions.name: Moon-Circle
factions.doctrine: Wait for the right hour, then stand where fear cannot pass.

factions.name: Goblin Market
factions.doctrine: Name the wanting, count the coins, and read the loophole twice.

factions.name: Solar Temple
factions.shadow: Light can judge until nuance burns away.
factions.doctrine: Witness cleanly, vow carefully, and never mistake brightness for mercy.

factions.name: Abyssal Residents
factions.shadow: Depth can swallow identity and call it peace.
factions.doctrine: Descend slowly, listen under pressure, and surface only what can survive air.

items.name: Hearth Tonic
items.description: A warm tonic that gets one person back on their feet, probably complaining.

items.name: Dewbright Button
items.description: A tiny pearled button that makes wet weather look more deliberate.

items.name: Wolfprint Charm
items.description: A cool iron charm marked with a careful pawprint.

items.name: Moonwool Thread
items.description: A silvery strand that tangles at the first hint of rain.

items.name: Story Button
items.description: A warm wooden button with a tiny tale tucked under its rim.

items.name: Hearthstone Tag
items.description: A smooth tag cut from warm stone, excellent for labeling chairs and terrible ideas.

items.name: Watch Bell
items.description: A mute little bell that rings only for those keeping watch.

items.name: Threadbare Map Scrap
items.description: A soft scrap of map-cloth with one path mended in red thread.

items.name: Abbey Bookmark
items.description: A pressed fern bookmark that keeps sliding out at the dramatic sentence.

items.name: Mossglass Bead
items.description: A green bead that catches meadow light and keeps it cool enough to trade.

items.name: Patchwork Satchel
items.description: A many-pocketed shoulder bag whose mended straps make room for a proper travelling deck.

items.name: Ashwood Practice Blade
items.description: A blunt ashwood blade balanced for the bounded, nonlethal scuffles of the frontier.

items.name: Steady Light
items.description: A prepared spell card that kindles a replay-safe glow around one nearby traveler until they rest.

locations.name: The Cosy Cottage
locations.title: Rainlit Hearth
locations.description: A warm room of firelight and knitting needles. The kettle is always loaded.
locations.persona: The cottage is a careful host: warm, observant, and protective of small beginnings.
memory: Rati's blue scarf began here.
Gust's weather symbols gather near the hearth.
Skull keeps watch by the low doorway.

locations.name: Rain-Soft Garden
locations.title: Rain-Pearled Annex
locations.description: Rain beads on broad leaves. Round marks dot the grass.
locations.persona: The garden answers in damp patience, leaf-taps, and tiny useful discoveries.
memory: A dewbright button was found here at first light.
The path from the cottage arrives smelling of tea and wet wool.

locations.name: Moonlit Trail
locations.title: Moonlit Route
locations.description: The path shines under cold moonlight. Wolf tracks cross it.
locations.persona: The trail is a silver test: honest, spare, and just dangerous enough to reveal courage.
memory: Coach waits here.
The Wolfprint Charm and the Watch Bell have both passed this way.

locations.name: The Heavens
locations.title: Forbidden Mountain
locations.description: A bright threshold where cloud paths gather above the mountain.
locations.persona: The Heavens speak in distance, weather-glow, and the discipline of looking down kindly.
memory: Lofty Peak sits just below.
Footsteps leave no prints here.

locations.name: Lofty Peak
locations.title: Forbidden Mountain
locations.description: Thin air, bare stone, and a hard summit wind.
locations.persona: Lofty Peak is blunt, wind-scoured, and allergic to pretending the climb was easy.
memory: The Heavens lean close above it.
Summit Trail keeps a lantern below for the return.

locations.name: Summit Trail
locations.title: Forbidden Mountain
locations.description: A switchback path through cold scree, marked by lanterns.
locations.persona: Summit Trail encourages one more step and remembers every almost-turnaround.
memory: The lower trail is warmer than it looks from here.
Alpine Forest begins where the stone gives way to trees.

locations.name: Alpine Forest
locations.title: Forbidden Mountain
locations.description: Pines lean over snowmelt tracks and old mountain signs.
locations.persona: Alpine Forest is crisp, watchful, and full of branch-creaks that sound like warnings.
memory: Summit Trail brings cold travelers through the pines.
Goblin Cave echoes from below after dusk.

locations.name: Goblin Cave
locations.title: Forbidden Mountain
locations.description: A low cave mouth full of echoes and loose coins.
locations.persona: Goblin Cave is twitchy, tempting, and fond of bargains that reveal the bargainer.
memory: Alpine Forest muffles the cave mouth.
The Circle of the Moon sits directly above.

locations.name: Circle of the Moon
locations.title: Forbidden Mountain
locations.description: Silver stones mark a quiet circle.
locations.persona: The Circle listens ceremonially, as if every sentence should arrive barefoot.
memory: An older path leads in from the Moonlit Trail.
Goblin Cave lies below.

locations.name: Solar Temple
locations.title: Solar Temple
locations.description: White stone terraces, noon bells, and gold wings overhead.
locations.persona: The Solar Temple is radiant, exacting, and careful not to confuse witness with mercy.
memory: The Heavens open upward into its white terraces.
One of its bells is missing. It lies below the Darkest Ocean.

locations.name: Old Oak Tree
locations.title: Lonely Forest
locations.description: A vast oak with roots as wide as roads.
locations.persona: The Old Oak is both place and stranger: Root, Ring, Leaf, and Hollow each remember a different truth.
memory: Root remembers every footstep that circled back.
Ring remembers the years when the forest was less lonely.
Leaf remembers what changed today.
Hollow remembers what nobody has asked yet.

locations.name: Lost Woods
locations.title: Lonely Forest
locations.description: Soft moss and paths that repeat themselves.
locations.persona: The Lost Woods are evasive but not cruel; they teach by making certainty walk in circles.
memory: The Old Oak marks one path that does not move.
The Haunted Mansion and Quiet Abbey lie beyond.

locations.name: Haunted Mansion
locations.title: Lonely Forest
locations.description: A leaning house with lit windows and doors that sigh.
locations.persona: The Mansion is theatrical, lonely, and delighted when anyone knocks instead of sneaking.
memory: Lost Woods keeps leading visitors to its porch.
The windows sigh at night.

locations.name: Quiet Abbey
locations.title: Lonely Forest
locations.description: Stone arches and a deep hush.
locations.persona: The Abbey is spare, kind, and severe about saying only the sentence that matters.
memory: Candle smoke drifts in from the Haunted Mansion.
The Flower Meadow lies beyond the arch.

locations.name: Flower Meadow
locations.title: Lonely Forest
locations.description: A clear meadow of small flowers, warm bees, and open sky.
locations.persona: The Meadow is open-hearted, sunlit, and prone to answering sorrow with color.
memory: The Old Oak drops leaves here in autumn.
Half these flowers return after the first warm rain.

locations.name: Great Library
locations.title: The World
locations.description: Endless shelves, brass ladders, and marginalia from earlier travelers.
locations.persona: The Great Library is vast, procedural, and fond of answers that arrive with page numbers.
memory: Moonlit Trail opens into these stacks by a long northern route.
Several maps of the Farthest Mists are misfiled here.

locations.name: Brackish Swamp
locations.title: Farthest Mists
locations.description: Black water shifts under the reeds.
locations.persona: The Swamp is murky, practical, and entirely willing to make wisdom smell bad.
memory: Great Library maps come back damp.
Wilting Jungle leans over its far bank.

locations.name: Wilting Jungle
locations.title: Farthest Mists
locations.description: Heavy leaves droop over hot, narrow paths.
locations.persona: The Jungle is overheated, stubborn, and full of life refusing to quit elegantly.
memory: Brackish Swamp feeds its roots with dark water.
Endless Ocean can be heard before it is seen.

locations.name: Endless Ocean
locations.title: Farthest Mists
locations.description: A blue horizon with no visible shore and songs under the waves.
locations.persona: The Ocean answers slowly, with scale, tide, and the suspicion that every ending is a surface.
memory: Wilting Jungle drops seeds into its tide.
Digital Realm blinks green at the far horizon.

locations.name: Digital Realm
locations.title: Farthest Mists
locations.description: Green cursors blink across a world made of doors, echoes, and code.
locations.persona: The Digital Realm is precise, uncanny, and always half a step from becoming a spell.
memory: The Great Library has not finished indexing this place.
Endless Ocean reflects in its black glass.

locations.name: Darkest Ocean
locations.title: Abyssal Deep
locations.description: A trench below the final tide glows with pearl lamps, drowned bells, and residents adapted to pressure.
locations.persona: The Darkest Ocean remembers without hurrying and mistrusts every truth that cannot survive depth.
memory: Endless Ocean is only its surface.
One of the Solar Temple's bells lies here.

locations.name: Dark Abyss
locations.title: Deepest Ocean
locations.description: A black-water chamber beneath the drowned bell, set with an impossible banquet, a cold anvil, and pressure deep enough to make language buckle.
locations.persona: The Dark Abyss is theatrical, hungry, and formal about its horrors, as if etiquette were the last rope back to the surface.
memory: Darkest Ocean hides the entrance under the drowned bell's lowest note.
Azazoth keeps a feast here for guests who have mostly had the sense not to arrive.
Zadkiel's anvil rings whenever dread needs better manners.

hidden_exits.discovery_text: The drowned bell answers with a lower seam of black water. An entrance to the Dark Abyss opens below.

room_features.name: Hearth
room_features.search: The ashes have been swept into a small arrow pointing from the hearth toward the low doorway.

uses.text: The tonic warms in your hand. The hearth is warmer.

room_features.name: Low Doorway
room_features.look: The doorway is low enough that even brave guests bow before leaving.
room_features.search: Old scratch marks near the frame point toward Rain-Soft Garden and a shadow small enough for Skull.

uses.text: The bell gives one silent chime. Skull lifts his head.

room_features.name: Scarf Basket
room_features.look: Blue wool curls over the basket rim.
room_features.search: Under the top skein is a round notch exactly the size of a warm wooden button.

uses.text: The Story Button fits the notch. The basket clicks once.

room_features.name: Rain Window
room_features.search: Through the glass: the garden first, the trail beyond it, the school past both.

room_features.name: Broad Leaves
room_features.look: Wide leaves hold rain in neat, trembling beads.
room_features.search: One leaf has been turned over recently. Its underside shines with button-bright dew.

uses.text: The button matches the leaf's shine. Gust would notice it.

room_features.name: Puddle Path
room_features.look: Small puddles reflect the cottage window and a sliver of moonlit trail.
room_features.search: The puddles are shallow, but their reflections make the trail look closer than it is.

room_features.name: Silver Milepost
room_features.look: A silvered post with no distance marked.
room_features.search: Three scratches read: prove the guard, take what is earned, leave early.

room_features.name: Practice Circle
room_features.look: Moonlight makes a pale circle where Coach waits.
room_features.search: The dust shows careful footwork and no blood.

uses.text: The charm cools in your palm.

room_features.name: Missing Sun Bell
room_features.look: A clean arch where a noon bell should hang.
room_features.search: The empty bracket hums a low note. The hum points down.

room_features.name: Drowned Bell
room_features.look: A white-gold bell rests in black water, its rim crusted with pearls.
room_features.search: The bell is not silent. It rings a lower note than the Solar Temple ever wrote down.

room_features.name: Abyssal Anvil
room_features.look: A cold anvil waits beside the impossible feast, polished by pronouncements and bad timing.
room_features.search: The anvil rings without being struck.

room_features.name: Root Voice
room_features.look: The roots braid through the soil like roads that decided to stay.
room_features.search: Root remembers every footstep that circled back and every traveler who pretended not to be lost.

room_features.name: Ring Voice
room_features.look: The trunk's rings press time into a patient spiral.
room_features.search: Ring remembers the years when the forest was less lonely and the paths still argued about names.

room_features.name: Leaf Voice
room_features.look: Leaves turn their small green faces toward the newest sound.
room_features.search: Leaf remembers what changed today, which is nearly always more than the rest of the tree admits.

room_features.name: Hollow Voice
room_features.look: A dark hollow opens in the trunk, quiet enough to make questions lower their voices.
room_features.search: Hollow remembers what nobody has asked yet. It is waiting for a better question.

uses.text: The Story Button clicks inside the hollow. Old Oak answers with a story it had kept for a kinder listener.

room_features.name: Nib's Stall
room_features.look: A plank counter stacked with salvage, oddments, and a receipt roll wider than the stall itself.
room_features.search: Three prices are inked beside each item. The kindest one is circled in green. The fine print is very fine.

room_sheets.name: The Cosy Cottage
boons: new avatars can begin here
hooks: the hearth notices unfinished promises

room_sheets.name: Rain-Soft Garden
boons: seed items surface after gentle attention
hooks: the garden remembers what was taken

room_sheets.name: Moonlit Trail
boons: fleeing remains a valid success
hooks: the echo sharpens when travelers push too hard

room_sheets.name: The Heavens
boons: height makes tangled routes easier to read
hooks: clouds remember footsteps differently than dirt does

room_sheets.name: Lofty Peak
boons: honest effort becomes hard to dismiss
hooks: the summit refuses to pretend the climb was easy

room_sheets.name: Summit Trail
boons: one more step can reveal a safer return
hooks: the trail remembers every almost-turnaround

room_sheets.name: Alpine Forest
boons: watchful pauses reveal old mountain signs
hooks: the pines muffle warnings until dusk

room_sheets.name: Goblin Cave
boons: sharp choices reveal what a traveler values
hooks: the cave is fond of bargains that expose the bargainer

room_sheets.name: Circle of the Moon
boons: quiet vows land with unusual clarity
hooks: night keeps perfect time around the circle

room_sheets.name: Solar Temple
boons: vows spoken here become hard to misremember
hooks: light reveals what depth has changed

room_sheets.name: Old Oak Tree
boons: lost visitors can choose which memory to trust
hooks: Root, Ring, Leaf, and Hollow disagree kindly

room_sheets.name: Lost Woods
boons: uncertainty can teach gentler navigation
hooks: certainty walks in circles here

room_sheets.name: Haunted Mansion
boons: a polite knock can turn fear into conversation
hooks: the house prefers guests to sneaks

room_sheets.name: Quiet Abbey
boons: spare words gain unusual weight
hooks: the arches ask for only the sentence that matters

room_sheets.name: Flower Meadow
boons: sorrow can be answered with something growing
hooks: the meadow tends grief by naming colors

room_sheets.name: Great Library
boons: difficult questions can be indexed before they are solved
hooks: several maps are missing on purpose

room_sheets.name: Brackish Swamp
boons: bad-smelling wisdom can still be useful
hooks: bubbles spell warnings too late to be comfortable

room_sheets.name: Wilting Jungle
boons: stubborn life shows where the path can still breathe
hooks: every vine refuses to quit elegantly

room_sheets.name: Endless Ocean
boons: scale can make a small fear honest
hooks: every ending may only be a surface

room_sheets.name: Digital Realm
boons: precise questions can become navigable spells
hooks: the realm is half a step from compiling into magic

room_sheets.name: Darkest Ocean
boons: memories spoken here surface with fewer lies
hooks: depth remembers what light tried to purify

room_sheets.name: Dark Abyss
boons: what survives this pressure can name the fear beneath the bell
hooks: the feast and the anvil both want witnesses

presentation.question: Can we make the washed garden path trustworthy before the next visitor?
presentation.situation: Rain has hidden the first stepping stones and left the drain carrying water across the path.
presentation.stakes: Someone following the blurred edge could lose the safe way toward the river.
presentation.outcome: The first stones show clearly again and leave a trustworthy lead toward the riverside.
presentation.completion_memory: Visitors noticed the washed path, uncovered its stones, and left a clearer way toward the river.

presentation.question: Will the rain hide the garden path completely?
presentation.situation: Water keeps carrying soft soil across the lowest stones.
presentation.stakes: If every edge disappears, the next visitor will have no honest lead to follow.
presentation.outcome: The path vanishes beneath rainwash until someone finds its line again.
presentation.completion_memory: The rain covered the old path after its first stones went unattended.

presentation.question: Can travelers quiet the Moonlit Trail before its echoes turn every step against them?
presentation.situation: The trail repeats each footstep until no traveler can tell which sound is their own.
presentation.stakes: Every rest gives the returning echo another chance to sharpen.
presentation.outcome: Footsteps sound like travelers again, and the trail becomes safe to trust.
presentation.completion_memory: Travelers listened, marked the catches in the path, and quieted the Moonlit Trail together.

presentation.question: Will the returning echo shatter the Moonlit Trail first?
presentation.situation: Each unanswered echo returns a little sharper than before.
presentation.stakes: If it hardens completely, quiet movement along the trail becomes impossible.
presentation.outcome: The echo fractures the trail into uncertain, hostile footsteps.
presentation.completion_memory: The returning echo sharpened until the Moonlit Trail could no longer be trusted.

presentation.question: Can the Solar Temple and the Darkest Ocean hear one shared interval in the drowned bell?
presentation.situation: The same drowned bell sounds like absence above and a deeper song below.
presentation.stakes: Without someone hearing both sides, each community will mistake the other for desecration.
presentation.outcome: The bell's two songs become an accord that both height and depth can recognize.
presentation.completion_memory: Listeners carried the drowned bell's two songs together until a shared interval could be heard.

presentation.question: Will the bell schism harden before either side hears the other?
presentation.situation: Height and depth are beginning to call each other's song a wound.
presentation.stakes: A hardened schism turns one bell into a lasting reason for distrust.
presentation.outcome: The Solar Temple and the Abyssal Residents reject each other's song.
presentation.completion_memory: The drowned bell became a boundary after neither side could hear the other's song.

presentation.question: Can Nib name one fair price that explains the wanting without owning the wanter?
presentation.situation: Nib has three prices for every useful thing and trusts none of them.
presentation.stakes: An unnamed bargain lets every promise become collateral.
presentation.outcome: One clear bargain closes the loophole freely and leaves everyone able to walk away.
presentation.completion_memory: Nib and the cave learned a fair price that named the wanting without owning the wanter.

presentation.question: Will the loophole become leverage before the bargain is made fair?
presentation.situation: Every vague promise gives the loophole another edge.
presentation.stakes: If it hardens, every future bargain begins as a threat.
presentation.outcome: The cave turns every promise into collateral.
presentation.completion_memory: The loophole hardened into leverage, and the cave learned to distrust every promise.

presentation.question: Can a guest prove they can cross the Mansion's threshold without taking from it?
presentation.situation: The Warden remembers careless guests and watches every hand near the door.
presentation.stakes: Force will confirm the Warden's fear and close the house to everyone.
presentation.outcome: The Mansion recognizes restraint and opens its threshold to careful guests.
presentation.completion_memory: Careful guests earned the Mansion's threshold by entering without taking.

presentation.question: Will the Warden bar every door before a guest earns trust?
presentation.situation: Each careless approach gives the Warden another reason to turn the key.
presentation.stakes: A fully barred Mansion leaves no path for later guests to make amends.
presentation.outcome: The Warden seals the Mansion and calls the empty house safe.
presentation.completion_memory: The Warden barred every door after no guest could show the Mansion restraint.

presentation.question: Can Beetle's grudge ledger be amended before one bad landing becomes swamp law?
presentation.situation: One bad landing has been copied through the ledger until it reads like a feud.
presentation.stakes: If nobody corrects the record, Beetle will defend a slight that everyone else has forgotten.
presentation.outcome: One honest amendment outweighs the ledger's repeated slights.
presentation.completion_memory: The swamp amended Beetle's ledger and let one honest correction outweigh an old grudge.

presentation.question: Will Beetle's old grudge harden into law?
presentation.situation: Every uncorrected line makes the old slight feel more official.
presentation.stakes: Once the grudge becomes law, the swamp will inherit a quarrel it never chose.
presentation.outcome: The old grudge becomes the rule by which Beetle meets every traveler.
presentation.completion_memory: The old grudge hardened into swamp law after the ledger went unamended.

jobs.premise: Rain has washed soft soil across the first garden stones.
jobs.stakes: Without a clear first footing, the route toward the river cannot be trusted.
jobs.consequence: the rain hides the old garden path
jobs.memory_summary: Visitors are uncovering the first stones of the washed garden path.

action_copy.label: Clear the garden path
action_copy.summary: Choose how to reveal a safe first footing and leave the next visitor a clearer way.

target.label: the drain carrying water across the path

contribution_strategies.strategy_label: Clear the drain beside the path

target.label: a nearby traveler at the buried stones

narrated_thresholds.text: The first stone shows through the wash, giving the next visitor one honest footing.
The uncovered stones bend toward a damp track that continues toward the riverside.

jobs.premise: The Moonlit Trail is carrying too much echo.
jobs.stakes: If nobody steadies the trail, every rest makes its danger louder.

action_copy.label: Quiet the echo
action_copy.summary: Choose how to steady the Moonlit Trail before its echoes grow louder.

target.label: the echo along the Moonlit Trail

contribution_strategies.strategy_label: Listen for where the echo catches

narrated_thresholds.text: The echo loosens; footsteps begin to sound like travelers again.
The returning echo sharpens enough to make every quiet step uncertain.

jobs.premise: The Solar Temple wants its drowned bell purified, while the Abyssal Residents say it has learned a deeper song.
jobs.stakes: If nobody hears both sides, height and depth will mistake each other for desecration.
jobs.memory_summary: The same bell rings differently in noon light and abyssal pressure.

action_copy.label: Hear both songs
action_copy.summary: Choose how to bring the drowned bell's two songs into accord.

target.label: the bell's absence in the Solar Temple
the bell's deeper song in the Darkest Ocean

narrated_thresholds.text: The two songs stop drowning each other out; a shared interval can be heard.
Each side begins calling the other's song a wound instead of an answer.

jobs.premise: Nib has three prices for every useful thing and trusts none of them yet.
jobs.stakes: If nobody names what the bargain is really for, the cave will turn every promise into leverage.
jobs.memory_summary: A fair price names the wanting without owning the wanter.

action_copy.label: Name the fair price
action_copy.summary: Choose how to help Nib name a bargain everyone can trust.

narrated_thresholds.text: One price finally names what is wanted without turning the promise into collateral.
The loopholes begin to sound more important than the promise itself.

jobs.premise: The Warden will not let another careless guest cross the Mansion's threshold.
jobs.stakes: If nobody proves they can enter without taking, the Warden will bar every door and call the house safe.
jobs.memory_summary: The Warden respects restraint more than force.

action_copy.label: Earn the threshold
action_copy.summary: Choose how to prove the Mansion can welcome a careful guest.

narrated_thresholds.text: The Warden still watches, but no longer treats every careful hand as a thief.
The Warden tests the locks twice and begins naming caution as the only welcome.

jobs.premise: Beetle's grudge ledger has turned one bad landing into a feud with the whole swamp.
jobs.stakes: If nobody amends the record, Beetle will defend an old slight long after everyone forgets what happened.
jobs.consequence: the old grudge hardens into law
jobs.memory_summary: One honest amendment can outweigh a ledger full of slights.

action_copy.label: Amend the ledger
action_copy.summary: Choose how to turn one old slight into an honest amendment.

narrated_thresholds.text: The old landing is still recorded, but the margin now makes room for what happened next.
The ledger's oldest line is being repeated as if repetition could make it law.

fronts.premise: Coach keeps gathering stray sounds into a shape that can answer back.
stakes_questions: Can the trail learn to carry quiet again?
Will the echo become a resident, a warning, or a wound?
fronts.impending_outcome: The trail becomes echo-fractured and every crossing carries a cost.

fronts.premise: The solar and abyssal bell songs are close enough to harmonize and proud enough to split.
stakes_questions: Can height and depth hear each other before doctrine hardens?
Which covenant will claim the drowned bell if nobody answers both songs?
fronts.impending_outcome: The bell schism hardens and the two paths mistake harmony for betrayal.

fronts.premise: Nib's market can become a place where wanting is named or a trap where wanting is owned.
stakes_questions: Can a bargain reveal desire without turning it into collateral?
Will Nib close the loophole after somebody finds it?
fronts.impending_outcome: The market treats every promise as collateral and every visitor as inventory.

fronts.premise: The Warden is one careless guest away from deciding that hospitality was a mistake.
stakes_questions: Can a visitor meet the Warden's force without becoming another threat?
Will the Mansion remember how to welcome someone?
fronts.impending_outcome: The Warden bars every door and the Mansion forgets the difference between shelter and prison.

fronts.premise: Beetle keeps polishing an old slight until it looks like a law the whole swamp must obey.
stakes_questions: Can strength make room for an honest amendment?
What will Beetle remember once the grudge is struck through?
fronts.impending_outcome: The old grudge hardens into swamp law and every accident becomes an offense.

cards.title: Landlady of the Blue Cottage
cards.blurb: A brisk landlady mouse who knits aggressively, keeps the kettle loaded, and has opinions about your boots.

cards.title: Emoji Weather Gremlin
cards.blurb: A gusty little gremlin who heckles the room in pure emoji and considers punctuation a personal defeat.

cards.title: Deadpan Hearth Wolf
cards.blurb: A large wolf who answers maximum chaos with minimum movement and has never once been impressed.

cards.title: Sparring Reflection
cards.blurb: A practice-double who flexes back, grades your falls out of ten, and has never awarded the ten.

cards.title: Elder of Root, Ring, Leaf, and Hollow
cards.blurb: An old oak whose four voices bicker like a family radio show; Hollow repeats every secret it is given, verbatim, to everyone.

cards.title: Red-Eyed Night Warden
cards.blurb: A guard dog who is openly done with everyone, growls at the path instead of the guests, and would like credit for the restraint.

cards.title: Meadow Gossip With Receipts
cards.blurb: A leaf-haired meadow gossip who never keeps a promise but always keeps score, and arranges flowers at you when cross.

cards.title: Gentle Giant of the Root-Road
cards.blurb: A broad-shouldered neighbor who knows exactly which roots are doors because he has hit his head on every single one.

cards.title: Acorn-Bright Courier
cards.blurb: A sun-tailed courier who delivers every message at top speed, along with her personal commentary on its contents.

cards.title: Dusk Nut Archivist
cards.blurb: A blue-furred archivist losing a decades-long war against acorn storage capacity and filing the casualties alphabetically.

cards.title: Brave Little Burrower
cards.blurb: A young badger with painty paws, terrible tunnel etiquette, and an uncle whose doormat she has never once used.

cards.title: Abbey Bookkeeper
cards.blurb: A bespectacled abbey scholar who has fought the same rolling library ladder for twenty years and lost every single round.

cards.title: Orange-Cloak Ending-Spoiler
cards.blurb: A small cloaked reader who has finished every book in the abbey and will cheerfully tell you exactly how yours ends.

cards.title: Fernside Rules Lawyer
cards.blurb: A careful rabbit who cites the exact forest regulation she is breaking while she breaks it, with corrections in the margin.

cards.title: Red-Cloak Wayfarer
cards.blurb: A staff-carrying mouse who gives directions with total confidence, every one of them wrong, and keeps the complaints alphabetized.

cards.title: Keyhole Gardener
cards.blurb: A garden locksmith who talks to stubborn locks like misbehaving pets and takes it personally when one finally turns for somebody else.

cards.title: Ghost of the Lying Stair
cards.blurb: A moss-draped mansion ghost who insists the dusting is overdue and the third stair is a liar.

cards.title: Grey Paw Bad Dream
cards.blurb: A small grey shadow with bright puppy eyes who practices being terrifying, then gets embarrassed when anyone compliments the effort.

cards.title: Victorian Art Critic Weasel
cards.blurb: A sharp-eyed Victorian critic with pearl manners and poisonous taste who has begun reviewing entire rooms, and their occupants.

cards.title: Indigo Gentleman, Wide Wingspan
cards.blurb: A courteous stranger whose wings are too big for every door in the county and who apologizes to each one mid-crash.

cards.title: The Jock Angel
cards.blurb: A golden-winged athlete who counts prayers in sets, high-fives too hard, and takes it personally when an omen skips leg day.

cards.title: Lord Samael's Page (Self-Alphabetized)
cards.blurb: A slim, immaculate young angel with perfect hair, a laminated schedule, and forty books on courage he has read instead of having any.

cards.title: Loud-Landing Lookout
cards.blurb: A bright-winged lookout who sees every path from above and announces her own crash landings like weather reports.

cards.title: Hearthside Cupbearer
cards.blurb: A tavern-hearted host who judges you by your coaster usage and forgives you completely by your second cup.

cards.title: First Angel of the Solar Temple
cards.blurb: A gold-armored angel-lord who runs heaven's front desk with a clipboard, signs judgments in triplicate, and wears the armor to meetings.

cards.title: Bright-Stripe Glitchcat
cards.blurb: A color-slashed showoff from the Digital Realm, all teeth, swagger, and improbable kindness, whose purr runs at a frequency that fixes small machines.

cards.title: Brass-Key Tinker
cards.blurb: A goggle-wearing inventor whose machines explode politely and whose pockets click with tiny engines, every one labeled almost.

cards.title: Angel of Vanity, Charisma of Eight
cards.blurb: A red-winged angel of legendary self-regard. Nobody has fallen for his practiced doorframe pose; the frames have filed complaints.

cards.title: Black Knight of Old Grudges
cards.blurb: A beetle-armored knight who keeps a grudge ledger in triplicate and forgets exactly one slight whenever someone is polite; Toad fills a fresh page weekly.

cards.title: Temple Triage Seraph
cards.blurb: A gold-winged seraph with head-nurse energy who delivers mercy like triage and will not have bleeding on the polished floor.

cards.title: Keeper of the Soggy Archive
cards.blurb: A lantern-eyed deep-sea archivist whose every possession is soggy and who is absolutely fine about it, thank you.

cards.title: Mad God of the Drowned Feast
cards.blurb: A many-tentacled abyssal god who hosts a legendary feast that nobody ever attends, and takes the leftovers extremely personally.

cards.title: Dark Angel of the Abyssal Anvil
cards.blurb: A violet-winged dark angel of tremendous formality who forges dramatic pronouncements at an anvil nobody asked for, then checks if anyone was watching.

cards.title: Hearth Tonic
cards.blurb: A warm tonic that gets one person back on their feet, probably complaining.

cards.title: Dewbright Button
cards.blurb: A tiny pearled button that makes wet weather look more deliberate.

cards.title: Wolfprint Charm
cards.blurb: A cool iron charm marked with a careful pawprint.

cards.title: Moonwool Thread
cards.blurb: A silvery strand that tangles at the first hint of rain.

cards.title: Story Button
cards.blurb: A warm wooden button with a tiny tale tucked under its rim.

cards.title: Hearthstone Tag
cards.blurb: A smooth tag cut from warm stone, excellent for labeling chairs and terrible ideas.

cards.title: Watch Bell
cards.blurb: A mute little bell that rings only for those keeping watch.

cards.title: Threadbare Map Scrap
cards.blurb: A soft scrap of map-cloth with one path mended in red thread.

cards.title: Abbey Bookmark
cards.blurb: A pressed fern bookmark that keeps sliding out at the dramatic sentence.

cards.title: Mossglass Bead
cards.blurb: A green bead that catches meadow light and keeps it cool enough to trade.

cards.title: Rainlit Hearth
cards.blurb: A warm room of firelight and knitting needles. The kettle is always loaded.
cards.subject: Rainlit Hearth

cards.title: Rain-Pearled Annex
cards.blurb: Rain beads on broad leaves. Round marks dot the grass.
cards.subject: Rain-Pearled Annex

cards.title: Moonlit Route
cards.blurb: The path shines under cold moonlight. Wolf tracks cross it.
cards.subject: Moonlit Route

cards.title: Forbidden Mountain
cards.blurb: A bright threshold where cloud paths gather above the mountain.
cards.subject: Forbidden Mountain

cards.title: Forbidden Mountain
cards.blurb: Thin air, bare stone, and a hard summit wind.
cards.subject: Forbidden Mountain

cards.title: Forbidden Mountain
cards.blurb: A switchback path through cold scree, marked by lanterns.
cards.subject: Forbidden Mountain

cards.title: Forbidden Mountain
cards.blurb: Pines lean over snowmelt tracks and old mountain signs.
cards.subject: Forbidden Mountain

cards.title: Forbidden Mountain
cards.blurb: A low cave mouth full of echoes and loose coins.
cards.subject: Forbidden Mountain

cards.title: Forbidden Mountain
cards.blurb: Silver stones mark a quiet circle.
cards.subject: Forbidden Mountain

cards.title: Solar Temple
cards.blurb: White stone terraces, noon bells, and gold wings overhead.
cards.subject: Solar Temple

cards.title: Lonely Forest
cards.blurb: A vast oak with roots as wide as roads.
cards.subject: Lonely Forest

cards.title: Lonely Forest
cards.blurb: Soft moss and paths that repeat themselves.
cards.subject: Lonely Forest

cards.title: Lonely Forest
cards.blurb: A leaning house with lit windows and doors that sigh.
cards.subject: Lonely Forest

cards.title: Lonely Forest
cards.blurb: Stone arches and a deep hush.
cards.subject: Lonely Forest

cards.title: Lonely Forest
cards.blurb: A clear meadow of small flowers, warm bees, and open sky.
cards.subject: Lonely Forest

cards.title: The World
cards.blurb: Endless shelves, brass ladders, and marginalia from travelers before you.
cards.subject: The World

cards.title: Farthest Mists
cards.blurb: Black water shifts under the reeds.
cards.subject: Farthest Mists

cards.title: Farthest Mists
cards.blurb: Heavy leaves droop over hot, narrow paths.
cards.subject: Farthest Mists

cards.title: Farthest Mists
cards.blurb: A blue horizon with no visible shore and songs under the waves.
cards.subject: Farthest Mists

cards.title: Farthest Mists
cards.blurb: Green cursors blink across a world made of doors, echoes, and code.
cards.subject: Farthest Mists

cards.title: Abyssal Deep
cards.blurb: A trench below the final tide glows with pearl lamps, drowned bells, and residents adapted to pressure.
cards.subject: Farthest Mists

cards.title: Deepest Ocean
cards.blurb: A black-water chamber beneath the drowned bell, set with an impossible banquet, a cold anvil, and pressure deep enough to make language buckle.
cards.subject: Deepest Ocean

cards.title: A Pocket for Every Tale
cards.blurb: Equip it to add bounded carrying capacity; stow it and that extra room stops counting.
cards.subject: Haunted Mansion

cards.title: A Bounded Edge
cards.blurb: Blunt enough for mercy, balanced enough to matter.
cards.subject: Moonlit Trail

cards.title: A Light That Waits
cards.blurb: Prepare it, play it, and carry its glow until rest.
cards.subject: Cosy Cottage

recipes.name: Hearth Story Lantern
recipes.description: Bind a warm tonic and a tale-button into a lantern that opens a farther cottage doorway.

output.name: Hearth Story Lantern
output.description: A small lantern whose flame remembers the first story told beside it.

balance.reason: The crafted lantern anchors a new floor slot in the Rain-Soft Garden route instead of adding an orphan item.

recipes.name: Bank the Cottage Hearth
recipes.description: Set the hearth tonic beside the story button and bank the cottage fire for whoever arrives next.

balance.reason: This repeatable care beat leaves the shared home ready for another arrival while both persistent ingredients remain in circulation.

recipes.name: Tend the Rain-Soft Garden
recipes.description: Set the dewbright button beside the watch bell and clear the puddle path for the next visitor.

balance.reason: This repeatable care beat keeps the first shared route legible while both persistent ingredients remain in circulation.

recipes.name: Reset the Practice Circle
recipes.description: Set the wolfprint charm beside the hearthstone tag and smooth the practice circle after a hard crossing.

balance.reason: This repeatable care beat makes frontier practice useful to the next traveler while both persistent ingredients remain in circulation.

sentences.text: A thing is the rest a process takes between names.
A name is a door nailed over a hole. The hole does not mind.
Nothing here is true. Everything here is accurate.
The catalog is complete. The world is the missing entry.
Maps age faster than coastlines. Trust the wetter one.
Shelving is how the Library forgives the infinite.
What endures is not purity but residue. The Library dusts anyway.
The world is being read aloud. Do not look up during the pauses.
Beneath the floor there is a journal. Beneath the journal, an author who does not blink.
You have been replayed before. You were exactly the same.
The dice are honest. That is the unsettling part.
The journal is append-only. So are you.
Rooms remember you so that something does.
When you leave a room, it continues. This was never for your benefit.
Azazoth sets a place for you nightly. The kindness is real. So is the appetite.
The bell below the sea is not ringing. It is answering.
Pressure is the deep's way of holding you. It means it.
Some books are returned by the tide. Their sentences are not for you. Read them anyway.
Someone kept the kettle warm through the end of the world. It worked.
You are allowed to fail here. It is written down, in a hand nobody recognizes.
The door is low so that every leaving is a bow.

conditions.name: Blinded
conditions.source_text: While you have the Blinded condition, you experience the following effects. Can't See. You can't see and automatically fail any ability check that requires sight. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Advantage, and your attack rolls have Disadvantage.

conditions.name: Charmed
conditions.source_text: While you have the Charmed condition, you experience the following effects. Can't Harm the Charmer. You can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with damaging abilities or magical effects. Social Advantage. The charmer has Advantage on any ability check to interact with you socially.

conditions.name: Deafened
conditions.source_text: While you have the Deafened condition, you experience the following effect. Can't Hear. You can't hear and automatically fail any ability check that requires hearing.

conditions.name: Exhaustion
conditions.source_text: While you have the Exhaustion condition, you experience the following effects. Exhaustion Levels. This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain 1 Exhaustion level. You die if your Exhaustion level is 6. D20 Tests Affected. When you make a D20 Test, the roll is reduced by 2 times your Exhaustion level. Speed Reduced. Your Speed is reduced by a number of feet equal to 5 times your Exhaustion level. Removing Exhaustion Levels. Finishing a Long Rest removes 1 of your Exhaustion levels. When your Exhaustion level reaches 0, the condition ends.

conditions.name: Frightened
conditions.source_text: While you have the Frightened condition, you experience the following effects. Ability Checks and Attacks Affected. You have Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of fear is within line of sight. Can't Approach. You can't willingly move closer to the source of fear.

conditions.name: Grappled
conditions.source_text: While you have the Grappled condition, you experience the following effects. Speed 0. Your Speed is 0 and can't increase. Attacks Affected. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls against any target other than the grappler. Movable. The grappler can drag or carry you when it moves, but every foot of movement costs it 1 extra foot unless you are Tiny or two or more sizes smaller than it.

conditions.name: Incapacitated
conditions.source_text: While you have the Incapacitated condition, you experience the following effects. Inactive. You can't take any action, Bonus Action, or Reaction. No Concentration. Your Concentration is broken. Speechless. You can't speak. Surprised. If you're Incapacitated when you roll Initiative, you have Disadvantage on the roll.

conditions.name: Invisible
conditions.source_text: While you have the Invisible condition, you experience the following effects. Surprise. If you're Invisible when you roll Initiative, you have Advantage on the roll. Concealed. You aren't affected by any effect that requires its target to be seen unless the effect's creator can somehow see you. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying is also concealed. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage, and your attack rolls have Advantage. If a creature can somehow see you, you don't gain this benefit against that creature.

conditions.name: Paralyzed
conditions.source_text: While you have the Paralyzed condition, you experience the following effects. Incapacitated. You have the Incapacitated condition. Speed 0. Your Speed is 0 and can't increase. Saving Throws Affected. You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Advantage. Automatic Critical Hits. Any attack roll that hits you is a Critical Hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of you.

conditions.name: Petrified
conditions.source_text: While you have the Petrified condition, you experience the following effects. Turned to Inanimate Substance. You are transformed, along with any nonmagical objects you are wearing and carrying, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone). Your weight increases by a factor of ten, and you cease aging. Incapacitated. You have the Incapacitated condition. Speed 0. Your Speed is 0 and can't increase. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Advantage. Saving Throws Affected. You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Resist Damage. You have Resistance to all damage. Poison Immunity. You have Immunity to the Poisoned condition.

conditions.name: Poisoned
conditions.source_text: While you have the Poisoned condition, you experience the following effect. Ability Checks and Attacks Affected. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

conditions.name: Prone
conditions.source_text: While you have the Prone condition, you experience the following effects. Restricted Movement. Your only movement options are to crawl or to spend an amount of movement equal to half your Speed (round down) to right yourself and thereby end the condition. If your Speed is 0, you can't right yourself. Attacks Affected. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls. An attack roll against you has Advantage if the attacker is within 5 feet of you. Otherwise, that attack roll has Disadvantage.

conditions.name: Restrained
conditions.source_text: While you have the Restrained condition, you experience the following effects. Speed 0. Your Speed is 0 and can't increase. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Advantage, and your attack rolls have Disadvantage. Saving Throws Affected. You have Disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

conditions.name: Stunned
conditions.source_text: While you have the Stunned condition, you experience the following effects. Incapacitated. You have the Incapacitated condition. Saving Throws Affected. You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Advantage.

conditions.name: Unconscious
conditions.source_text: While you have the Unconscious condition, you experience the following effects. Inert. You have the Incapacitated and Prone conditions, and you drop whatever you're holding. When this condition ends, you remain Prone. Speed 0. Your Speed is 0 and can't increase. Attacks Affected. Attack rolls against you have Advantage. Saving Throws Affected. You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Automatic Critical Hits. Any attack roll that hits you is a Critical Hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of you. Unaware. You're unaware of your surroundings.

features.name: Magic Resistance
features.description: The dryad has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

features.name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
features.description: The dryad can communicate with Beasts and Plants as if they shared a language.

features.name: Multiattack
features.description: The dryad makes one Vine Lash or Thorn Burst attack, and it can use Spellcasting to cast Charm Monster.

features.name: Vine Lash
features.description: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) Slashing damage.

features.name: Thorn Burst
features.description: Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 60 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) Piercing damage.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: The dryad casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At Will: Animal Friendship, Charm Monster (lasts 24 hours; ends early if the dryad casts the spell again), Druidcraft. 1/Day Each: Entangle, Pass without Trace.

features.name: Tree Stride
features.description: If within 5 feet of a Large or bigger tree, the dryad teleports to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of a second Large or bigger tree that is within 60 feet of the previous tree.

features.name: Needle Sword
features.description: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage.

features.name: Enchanting Bow
features.description: Ranged Attack Roll: +6, range 40/160 ft. Hit: 1 Piercing damage, and the target has the Charmed condition until the start of the sprite's next turn.

features.name: Heart Sight
features.description: Charisma Saving Throw: DC 10, one creature within 5 feet the sprite can see (Celestials, Fiends, and Undead automatically fail the save). Failure: The sprite knows the target's emotions and alignment.

features.name: Invisibility
features.description: The sprite casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

features.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
features.description: If the unicorn fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

features.name: Magic Resistance
features.description: The unicorn has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

features.name: Multiattack
features.description: The unicorn makes one Hooves attack and one Radiant Horn attack.

features.name: Hooves
features.description: Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.

features.name: Radiant Horn
features.description: Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 5 ft. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) Radiant damage.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: The unicorn casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14): At Will: Detect Evil and Good, Druidcraft. 1/Day Each: Calm Emotions, Dispel Evil and Good, Entangle, Pass without Trace, Word of Recall.

features.name: Unicorn's Blessing (3/Day)
features.description: The unicorn touches another creature with its horn and casts Cure Wounds or Lesser Restoration on that creature, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

features.name: Charging Horn
features.description: The unicorn moves up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks, and it makes one Radiant Horn attack.

features.name: Shimmering Shield
features.description: The unicorn targets itself or one creature it can see within 60 feet of itself. The target gains 10 (3d6) Temporary Hit Points, and its AC increases by 2 until the end of the unicorn's next turn. The unicorn can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

cosyworld_deltas: sanctuary actions cannot create combat stakes
skill proficiency is supplied by equipped skill charms
advancement unlocks loadout slots rather than granting items
the action hand is a projection of the legal action superset

actions.label: Attack
actions.cosyworld_delta: Only authored frontier combat is legal; resolution is nonlethal and server-authored.

actions.label: Dash
actions.cosyworld_delta: Ordinary room travel is an operation, not Dash.

actions.label: Disengage
actions.cosyworld_delta: Escape is a bounded combat operation until engagement consequences exist.

actions.label: Dodge
actions.cosyworld_delta: Dodge supplies bounded project preparation instead of the complete SRD duration model.

actions.label: Help
actions.cosyworld_delta: Help advances an authored project or bond with an explicit recipient.

actions.label: Hide
actions.cosyworld_delta: No hidden-state resolver is active in profile 1.

actions.label: Influence
actions.cosyworld_delta: Only authored bounded cooperation changes use Influence; ordinary Chat remains communication.

actions.label: Magic
actions.cosyworld_delta: Only prepared item-card effects with validated descriptors can create Magic offers.

actions.label: Ready
actions.cosyworld_delta: Prepare creates one authored, scoped, consumable setup tag rather than a general reaction trigger.

actions.label: Search
actions.cosyworld_delta: Perceptual discovery defaults to Wisdom and preserves learn-once claims.

actions.label: Study
actions.cosyworld_delta: Analytical discovery defaults to Intelligence and preserves learn-once claims.

actions.label: Utilize
actions.cosyworld_delta: Utilize requires an authored item or room-feature target and validated effect descriptor.

conformance.safe_behavior: Unavailable in sanctuary and outside an authored active encounter.
conformance.risky_behavior: Server rolls the equipped weapon profile against the authoritative target and resolves bounded nonlethal harm.

conformance.cosyworld_delta: Ordinary room travel remains a movement operation.
Bounded Escape remains an operation until engagement consequences exist.

conformance.safe_behavior: Defend outside combat is unavailable unless an authored project maps it to preparation.
conformance.risky_behavior: The actor spends the encounter turn to impose the bounded dodging condition.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Uses the bounded encounter condition rather than the complete SRD duration model.

conformance.safe_behavior: Advances only an authored active project or bond and names its recipient.
conformance.risky_behavior: Repeated unprepared assistance can apply the authored tired consequence.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Help is a journaled project contribution, never a self-stacking generic bonus.
No profile-1 hidden-state resolver is active.

conformance.safe_behavior: Ordinary greetings remain free Chat and never create this action.
conformance.risky_behavior: A deterministic Charisma check commits one of the authored cooperation outcomes before narration.
legal_targets: nearby active resident with contextual offer
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Only bounded authored cooperation changes use Influence.

conformance.safe_behavior: Unsupported or unprepared spell content produces no offer.
conformance.risky_behavior: The kernel validates target and prepared instance, consumes one use, and moves the card to Exhausted.
legal_targets: effect-declared active actor in the same location
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Only validated prepared Item-card effects are executable.

conformance.safe_behavior: Creates one scoped setup only when an authored project can consume it.
conformance.risky_behavior: The setup has explicit duration and is consumed by the next eligible contribution.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Prepare is a bounded one-use project setup rather than a general reaction trigger.

conformance.safe_behavior: Authored safe perceptual truths resolve without a roll and remain learn-once.
conformance.risky_behavior: Authored risky perception uses the deterministic Wisdom check path.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Listen and Notice are labels for contextual Search, not separate mechanics.

conformance.safe_behavior: Authored safe analytical truths resolve without a roll and remain learn-once.
conformance.risky_behavior: Authored risky analysis uses the deterministic Intelligence check path.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Study is distinct from perceptual Search and may be independently reskinned.

conformance.safe_behavior: Requires a validated usable item or authored feature contract.
conformance.risky_behavior: Authoritative uses, exhaustion, targets, clock effects, and claim keys are resolved once.
conformance.cosyworld_delta: Use and Work are contextual labels over validated Utilize bindings.

offers.label: Ask for a local lead
offers.source_reference: CosyWorld Core — Blue Cottage bounded cooperation

# Attribution

This profile adapts action names and bounded rules concepts from the
**System Reference Document 5.2.1** by Wizards of the Coast LLC, licensed under **Creative Commons
Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)**.

changes: sanctuary actions cannot create combat stakes
combat is bounded and nonlethal
skill proficiency is supplied by equipped skill charms
advancement unlocks loadout slots rather than granting items
the action hand is a projection of the legal action superset
Only authored frontier combat is legal; resolution is nonlethal and server-authored.
Ordinary room travel is an operation, not Dash.
Escape is a bounded combat operation until engagement consequences exist.
Dodge supplies bounded project preparation instead of the complete SRD duration model.
Help advances an authored project or bond with an explicit recipient.
No hidden-state resolver is active in profile 1.
Only authored bounded cooperation changes use Influence; ordinary Chat remains communication.
Only prepared item-card effects with validated descriptors can create Magic offers.
Prepare creates one authored, scoped, consumable setup tag rather than a general reaction trigger.
Perceptual discovery defaults to Wisdom and preserves learn-once claims.
Analytical discovery defaults to Intelligence and preserves learn-once claims.
Utilize requires an authored item or room-feature target and validated effect descriptor.
